propositions: The README quickstart claims a single end-to-end run across three surfaces but does not actually deliver one.
The CLI block creates a ticket then acts on an unrelated hard-coded id (reel-lot-tea) instead of the created ticket.
The Python block creates a separate ticket rather than threading the same one, and the MCP block only shows client config without performing an operation.
The CLI quickstart must be a runnable single-ticket sequence that captures the created id and threads it through create, ready, claim, and close with no disconnected hard-coded id.
Sibling ticket 0435 extracts and executes the CLI fenced block verbatim in CI, requiring one coherent runnable command sequence.
The MCP block prose must stop implying it performs an operation and instead present it as server config.
